symptoms.symptom_name: Periumbilical pain migrating to the right iliac fossa
symptoms.character: dull central ache migrating to sharp RIF cramps

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic migratory prodrome of appendicitis; the single most important reason it cannot be excluded on history.
Terminal ileitis produces RIF pain, but migration from the umbilicus is less typical than for appendicitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intermittent crampy pain relieved by lying still

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Colicky, remitting pain fits bowel-wall spasm of a flare.
Appendicitis pain is typically constant and progressive rather than intermittent — softens but does not exclude the diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Two loose unformed stools this evening, no blood

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loose stools are consistent with active terminal ileitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent identical RIF twinges on a background of known chronic bowel inflammation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent stereotyped episodes on a known chronic bowel inflammation strongly favour a flare of the established condition.
Prior identical episodes make a first-time surgical appendicitis less likely — but a patient with IBD can still get appendicitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No anorexia, no persistent vomiting, no rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anorexia and vomiting are common in appendicitis; their absence lowers probability but does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised mild tenderness to deep palpation in the right iliac fossa

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised ileocecal tenderness; sealed clinician finding not obtainable via telehealth.
RIF tenderness is common to both — non-discriminating without the peritoneal signs.
Absence of peritonism is a key reason the source clinician excluded a surgical abdomen — a finding Breath-Ezy cannot elicit.
Negative Rovsing's lowers appendicitis probability; sealed clinician manoeuvre.
Equivocal — neither confirms nor excludes; sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Active bowel sounds, no palpable mass

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Active bowel sounds and no mass argue against obstruction or abscess; sealed auscultation/palpation finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sharp exacerbation on cough/movement; abdomen not board-rigid

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-reportable proxies for peritonism are currently negative — reassuring but not a substitute for examination.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Flare activity not objectively confirmed; monitoring baseline missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sensitive marker of intestinal inflammation supporting an active flare; not available in real time via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Mildly elevated in a mild flare; markedly elevated would raise concern for a complication or surgical process

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Supports inflammation and helps gauge severity; a high CRP would argue for urgent surgical assessment.
Afebrile lowers probability of an established suppurative process; patient-obtainable via home thermometer, unverified, and does not exclude early appendicitis.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Intermittent crampy RIF pain with loose stools, afebrile and no anorexia, on a background of recurrent identical episodes — the composite favouring a mild flare over a surgical abdomen.
Migratory RIF pain with local tenderness keeps appendicitis live; the discriminating peritoneal signs are examination-only, so remote exclusion is impossible.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important step — a clinician must palpate the abdomen to assess for peritonism and exclude appendicitis, which telehealth cannot do.
Objectively confirms intestinal inflammation / flare activity; arranged via the GP follow-up.
Gauge inflammatory severity; a markedly raised CRP or WCC would prompt urgent surgical assessment.
Imaging is an in-person / Emergency Department decision if appendicitis is clinically suspected on examination — the AI Doctor should not order or promise imaging remotely.
